briefdescription.para: Marks dirty buffers that need to be uploaded to OpenGL.
Returns true if something was rendered, or false if nothing to render.

detaileddescription.para: These functions do: 1) bind data 2) pass shaders arguments 3) draw data

Those dummy definitions remove undefined references in RenderObjectCombinator when it calls non-overridden pure virtual methods. We could check in RenderObjectCombinator if they're overridden or not, but it's easier to just define them.

briefdescription.para: returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap
returns the amount of memory this object allocated in OpenGL
binds all data for this render object, not to bind ever again (until object becomes dirty)

memberdef.argsstring: (const ModelRenderParams &parameters, RenderModelPassMask desiredPass, GLuint vao, const Color &color, uint32_t dirtyFlag)
memberdef.name: renderEdges_

memberdef.argsstring: (bool solid, ViewportId viewportId, bool picker=false) const
memberdef.name: drawMesh_

briefdescription.para: Create a new set of OpenGL buffer objects.
returns dirty flag of currently using normal type if they are dirty in render representation
